---
format: html
title: make_trait_table()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>make_trait_table</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Make Wide-Format Species x Traits Table</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Pivots a long-format aggregated trait data frame into a wide-format
table with one row per taxon and one column per trait domain. This
produces the genus x traits matrix used for functional type
assignment.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>make_trait_table(
  data,
  taxon_col = "taxon_name",
  trait_col = "trait_domain_name",
  value_col = "trait_value_aggregated"
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data">data</code></td>
<td>
<p>A data frame in long format with one row per taxon x trait
combination. Must contain at least the columns specified by
'taxon_col', 'trait_col', and 'value_col'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="taxon_col">taxon_col</code></td>
<td>
<p>A character string naming the column containing taxon (genus) names.
Default: '&quot;taxon_name&quot;'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="trait_col">trait_col</code></td>
<td>
<p>A character string naming the column containing trait domain names.
Default: '&quot;trait_domain_name&quot;'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="value_col">value_col</code></td>
<td>
<p>A character string naming the column containing aggregated trait
values. Default: '&quot;trait_value_aggregated&quot;'.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>Wraps 'tidyr::pivot_wider()'. If a taxon x trait combination appears
more than once, only the first value is retained
('values_fn = dplyr::first').
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A tibble with one row per taxon and one column per trait domain,
plus the taxon name column. Column names for traits are taken
directly from the values in 'trait_col'.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[aggregate_trait_values()], [check_trait_coverage()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
